# upcloud_managed_object_storage_static_site (Resource)

This resource represents an UpCloud Managed Object Storage static site.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "upcloud_managed_object_storage" "this" {
  name              = "example"
  region            = "europe-1"
  configured_status = "started"
}

resource "upcloud_managed_object_storage_static_site" "this" {
  service_uuid = "1201cd6f-20cd-44b6-939d-ae1c861769e7"

  bucket_name   = "example"
  bucket_prefix = "public/"

  error_pages = [{
    error_document = "404.html"
    status_code    = 404
  }]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required Attributes

- `bucket_name` (String) S3 bucket from which static content is served. Must have a public read policy.
- `service_uuid` (String) Managed Object Storage service UUID.

### Optional Attributes

- `bucket_prefix` (String) Path prefix within the bucket. For example, `dist/` serves content from the `dist/` folder. Defaults to empty string (bucket root).
- `domain_name` (String) A custom domain in `static-website` mode attached to the service.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable or disable serving content on this domain. Defaults to true.
- `error_pages` (Attributes List) Custom error pages served when the storage backend returns an error status. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--error_pages))
- `index_document` (String) Name of the index document. Defaults to `index.html`.
- `spa_mode` (Boolean) Whether to enable single-page application mode. In SPA mode, the index document is served for all paths, which is useful for client-side routing.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) ID of the static site. ID is in {object storage UUID}/{domain name} format.

<a id="nestedatt--error_pages"></a>
### Nested Schema for `error_pages`

Required Attributes:

- `error_document` (String) Path to the error page document within the bucket.

Optional Attributes:

- `status_code` (Number) Exact HTTP status code to match. Mutually exclusive with status range.
- `status_range_end` (Number) End of the status code range (inclusive). Must be greater than start.
- `status_range_start` (Number) Start of the status code range (inclusive).
